nodes.name: 圣菲省
nodes.summary: 圣菲省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括白枕霸鹟、Stripe-backed antbird、Great rufous woodcreeper等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Hermann Burmeister

nodes.name: 南半球
nodes.summary: 南半球是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括Grey petrel、Great-winged petrel、Scissor-tailed nightjar等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Fauna of South America
nodes.summary: Fauna of South America是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白枕霸鹟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 白枕霸鹟
nodes.summary: 白枕霸鹟（White-naped xenopsaris，学名 Xenopsaris albinucha），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联8个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括稀树草原、灌丛。

nodes.name: 羅伯特·里奇韋
nodes.summary: 羅伯特·里奇韋是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括小加拿大雁、乌镰翅鸡、Blue-crowned parakeet等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The white-naped xenopsaris (; Xenopsaris albinucha), also known as the reed becard and white-naped becard, is a species of suboscine bird in the family Tityridae, the only member of the genus Xenopsaris

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in South America, in humid subtropical and tropical savanna climates in most of the countries east of the Andes: Venezuela, Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Argentina

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in South America, in humid subtropical and tropical savanna climates in most of the countries east of the Andes: Venezuela, Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Argentina

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The subspecies X. a. minor is found in west and central Venezuela, and probably extends into north-eastern Colombia. The species is generally resident across its range, but sightings of solitary and silent birds have suggested that the species may be migratory in Bolivia and Brazil. A study published in 2005 suggested it was migratory in Santa Fe, Argentina, as the species was not observed in the area between March and September (the austral winter). In 2006 the species was reported for the first time in Peru, but it was unclear if this represented a vagrant escaping cold weather or a migrant, as the species is mostly uncommon across its range and that area is poorly studied ornithologically. They occupy a variety of habitats across their range, including Caatinga scrubland, riparian (river) woodland, lightly wooded areas, the borders of open gallery forest and open areas with scattered trees
